title: pilosella ziziana subsp. multifida
intro: Pilosella ziziana subsp. multifida, also known as a subspecies of the knapweed or false hawk's beard, is a perennial plant native to Europe, typically found in dry, rocky areas. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which can aid in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Pilosella ziziana subsp. multifida are said to include relief from digestive problems, colds, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant. Herbal preparations of this plant are often found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, where it's used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms associated with various conditions.
